# bi — The 2-Letter Dev Toolkit (PRD v1.0.0)

> **Tagline:** Type less, do more.
> **npm:** `bi` | **GitHub:** `10iii/bi` | **Author:** `10iii <zh@ngxil.in>`

---

## 1. Problem Statement

JavaScript developers juggle 4+ package managers (`npm`, `yarn`, `pnpm`, `bun`) with incompatible CLI commands. When switching between projects, you must remember whether to run `npm install`, `yarn add`, `pnpm add`, or `bun add`. AI coding agents face the same problem — they frequently guess the wrong package manager and break workflows.

**`bi` solves this** by providing a single 2-letter command that auto-detects the package manager and proxies all operations through a unified interface.

## 2. Core Design Principles

1. **Zero Config:** Auto-detect everything from lockfiles and config files.
2. **Human + AI Dual Mode:** Human mode shows colored TUI output; `--json` flag outputs structured JSON for AI agents.
3. **Proxy, Don't Re-implement:** `bi` does NOT re-implement npm/yarn/pnpm/bun internals. It detects the correct tool and spawns it as a child process.
4. **Fail Loud:** If detection fails, exit with clear error code and message. Never guess silently.

## 3. Technology Stack

- **Language:** TypeScript
- **Build:** tsup (CJS output, target node18)
- **Test:** vitest
- **Dependencies:** Zero runtime dependencies (stdlib only: `child_process`, `fs`, `path`, `os`)

## 4. File Structure

```
bi/
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
├── tsup.config.ts
├── vitest.config.ts
├── README.md
├── src/
│   ├── cli.ts                  # Entry point, argument parser
│   ├── detect.ts               # Package manager detection engine
│   ├── commands/
│   │   ├── install.ts          # bi i [pkg...]
│   │   ├── remove.ts           # bi rm <pkg...>
│   │   ├── run.ts              # bi run <script>  (aliases: bi dev, bi build, bi test)
│   │   ├── exec.ts             # bi x <pkg> [args]
│   │   ├── upgrade.ts          # bi up
│   │   ├── clean.ts            # bi clean
│   │   └── init.ts             # bi init (interactive scaffolding)
│   └── utils/
│       ├── spawn.ts            # Child process wrapper
│       └── output.ts           # Human vs JSON output formatting
└── tests/
    ├── detect.test.ts
    ├── install.test.ts
    ├── remove.test.ts
    ├── run.test.ts
    ├── exec.test.ts
    ├── upgrade.test.ts
    ├── clean.test.ts
    ├── init.test.ts
    ├── cli.test.ts
    └── spawn.test.ts
```

---

## 5. Global Flags

| Flag | Alias | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `--help` | `-h` | Print help summary showing all commands and usage examples. Exit 0. |
| `--version` | `-v` | Print `bi@<version>` and exit 0. |
| `--json` | — | Switch all output to structured JSON (for AI agents). |

**`--help` output (human):**
```
bi v1.0.0 — The 2-letter dev toolkit. Type less, do more.

Usage: bi <command> [options]

Commands:
  bi i [pkg...]         Install dependencies (aliases: install, add)
  bi rm <pkg...>        Remove dependencies (aliases: remove, un)
  bi run <script>       Run a script (shortcuts: bi dev, bi build, bi test)
  bi x <pkg> [args]     Execute a package (alias: exec)
  bi up                 Check and upgrade outdated dependencies
  bi clean              Delete node_modules and caches
  bi init               Scaffold a new project

Global Flags:
  -h, --help            Show this help message
  -v, --version         Show version number
  --json                Output as JSON (for AI agents)

Detected: pnpm (from pnpm-lock.yaml)
```

**`--help` output (json, with `--json --help`):**
```json
{
  "name": "bi",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "commands": [
    { "name": "i", "aliases": ["install","add"], "usage": "bi i [pkg...]", "desc": "Install dependencies" },
    { "name": "rm", "aliases": ["remove","un"], "usage": "bi rm <pkg...>", "desc": "Remove dependencies" },
    { "name": "run", "aliases": ["dev","build","test","start"], "usage": "bi run <script>", "desc": "Run a script" },
    { "name": "x", "aliases": ["exec"], "usage": "bi x <pkg>", "desc": "Execute a package" },
    { "name": "up", "aliases": ["upgrade"], "usage": "bi up", "desc": "Upgrade outdated deps" },
    { "name": "clean", "aliases": ["purge"], "usage": "bi clean", "desc": "Delete node_modules and caches" },
    { "name": "init", "aliases": [], "usage": "bi init", "desc": "Scaffold a new project" }
  ],
  "detected_pm": { "pm": "pnpm", "reason": "lockfile" }
}
```

---

## 6. Command Reference

### 6.1 `bi` (bare, no arguments)
- Same as `bi --help`. Print help summary.

### 6.2 `bi i [packages...]` — Install
**Aliases:** `bi install`, `bi add`

| Scenario | npm | yarn | pnpm | bun |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `bi i` (no args) | `npm install` | `yarn install` | `pnpm install` | `bun install` |
| `bi i lodash` | `npm install lodash` | `yarn add lodash` | `pnpm add lodash` | `bun add lodash` |
| `bi i -D vitest` | `npm install -D vitest` | `yarn add -D vitest` | `pnpm add -D vitest` | `bun add -d vitest` |
| `bi i -g tsx` | `npm install -g tsx` | `yarn global add tsx` | `pnpm add -g tsx` | `bun add -g tsx` |

**Flags:**
- `-D` / `--dev`: devDependency
- `-g` / `--global`: global install
- `--json`: output result as JSON

**Function Signature:**
```typescript
// src/commands/install.ts
export async function install(
  packages: string[],
  options: { dev?: boolean; global?: boolean; json?: boolean }
): Promise<{ exitCode: number; pm: string; command: string }>;
```

### 6.3 `bi rm <packages...>` — Remove
**Aliases:** `bi remove`, `bi un`, `bi uninstall`

| Scenario | npm | yarn | pnpm | bun |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `bi rm lodash` | `npm uninstall lodash` | `yarn remove lodash` | `pnpm remove lodash` | `bun remove lodash` |

**Function Signature:**
```typescript
// src/commands/remove.ts
export async function remove(
  packages: string[],
  options: { json?: boolean }
): Promise<{ exitCode: number; pm: string; command: string }>;
```

### 6.4 `bi run <script>` — Run Script
**Aliases:** `bi dev` = `bi run dev`, `bi build` = `bi run build`, `bi test` = `bi run test`, `bi start` = `bi run start`

| Scenario | npm | yarn | pnpm | bun |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `bi run dev` | `npm run dev` | `yarn dev` | `pnpm dev` | `bun run dev` |
| `bi dev` | Same as above | | | |

**Function Signature:**
```typescript
// src/commands/run.ts
export async function run(
  script: string,
  args: string[],
  options: { json?: boolean }
): Promise<{ exitCode: number; pm: string; command: string }>;
```

### 6.5 `bi x <package> [args...]` — Execute Package
**Aliases:** `bi exec`

| Scenario | npm | yarn | pnpm | bun |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `bi x vitest` | `npx vitest` | `yarn dlx vitest` | `pnpm dlx vitest` | `bunx vitest` |

**Function Signature:**
```typescript
// src/commands/exec.ts
export async function exec(
  pkg: string,
  args: string[],
  options: { json?: boolean }
): Promise<{ exitCode: number; pm: string; command: string }>;
```

### 6.6 `bi up` — Upgrade Dependencies
**Aliases:** `bi upgrade`, `bi update`

**Behavior:**
1. Read `package.json` dependencies + devDependencies.
2. For each dependency, query the npm registry for the latest version.
3. Compare with current pinned version.
4. Print a table of outdated packages with current vs latest.
5. In human mode, prompt to auto-update `package.json`. In `--json` mode, just output the diff.

**Function Signature:**
```typescript
// src/commands/upgrade.ts
export interface OutdatedPackage {
  name: string;
  current: string;
  latest: string;
  type: 'dependencies' | 'devDependencies';
}

export async function upgrade(
  options: { json?: boolean; apply?: boolean }
): Promise<{ outdated: OutdatedPackage[]; updated: number }>;
```

### 6.7 `bi clean` — Clean Project
**Aliases:** `bi purge`

**Behavior:**
1. Delete `node_modules/` directory.
2. Delete common cache directories: `.cache/`, `.next/`, `.nuxt/`, `dist/`, `.turbo/`.
3. Report total freed disk space.

**Function Signature:**
```typescript
// src/commands/clean.ts
export async function clean(
  options: { json?: boolean; dryRun?: boolean }
): Promise<{ deleted: string[]; freedBytes: number }>;
```

### 6.8 `bi init` — Scaffold New Project
**Behavior (v1.0 scope):**
1. Present a list of popular frameworks: React (Vite), Next.js, Vue, Nuxt, Svelte, Astro, Express, Fastify.
2. User selects one.
3. `bi` runs the corresponding official `create-*` command (e.g., `npm create vite@latest`).

In `--json` mode, list available scaffolds as JSON without interactive prompts.

**Function Signature:**
```typescript
// src/commands/init.ts
export interface Scaffold {
  name: string;
  command: string;
  description: string;
}

export async function init(
  options: { json?: boolean; template?: string }
): Promise<{ scaffold: Scaffold; exitCode: number }>;
```

---

## 7. Core Module: Package Manager Detection

**File:** `src/detect.ts`

**Detection Algorithm (priority order):**

1. Check for lockfiles in cwd (first match wins):
   - `bun.lockb` or `bun.lock` → `bun`
   - `pnpm-lock.yaml` → `pnpm`
   - `yarn.lock` → `yarn`
   - `package-lock.json` → `npm`
2. Check `package.json` → `packageManager` field (e.g., `"packageManager": "pnpm@8.15.0"`)
3. Fall back to checking which binaries are available on `$PATH` (in order: `bun`, `pnpm`, `yarn`, `npm`)
4. If nothing found, default to `npm` and warn.

**Function Signature:**
```typescript
// src/detect.ts
export type PackageManager = 'npm' | 'yarn' | 'pnpm' | 'bun';

export interface DetectionResult {
  pm: PackageManager;
  version: string | null;
  reason: 'lockfile' | 'packageManager-field' | 'path-lookup' | 'default';
  lockfile?: string;
}

export function detectPackageManager(cwd?: string): DetectionResult;
```

**Output Example (JSON mode):**
```json
{
  "pm": "pnpm",
  "version": "8.15.0",
  "reason": "lockfile",
  "lockfile": "pnpm-lock.yaml"
}
```

---

## 8. Utility Modules

### 8.1 `src/utils/spawn.ts` — Child Process Wrapper

```typescript
export interface SpawnResult {
  exitCode: number;
  stdout: string;
  stderr: string;
}

export function spawnPM(
  pm: PackageManager,
  args: string[],
  options?: { cwd?: string; stdio?: 'inherit' | 'pipe' }
): Promise<SpawnResult>;
```

**Key behavior:**
- By default, `stdio: 'inherit'` so the user sees real-time output.
- In `--json` mode, `stdio: 'pipe'` to capture output.
- On Windows, use `shell: true` to resolve `.cmd` wrappers.

### 8.2 `src/utils/output.ts` — Output Formatting

```typescript
export function printHuman(message: string, type?: 'info' | 'success' | 'warn' | 'error'): void;
export function printJSON(data: unknown): void;
export function isJsonMode(): boolean;
```

---

## 9. CLI Entry Point

**File:** `src/cli.ts`

```typescript
#!/usr/bin/env node

// Argument parsing (no external deps, use process.argv manually)
// Global flags (parsed FIRST, before command routing):
//   --help / -h           → printHelp(), exit 0
//   --version / -v        → print "bi@<version>", exit 0
//   --json                → set global JSON mode flag
//
// Command routing:
//   bi                    → help (same as --help)
//   bi i / install / add  → install()
//   bi rm / remove / un   → remove()
//   bi run / dev / build / test / start → run()
//   bi x / exec           → exec()
//   bi up / upgrade       → upgrade()
//   bi clean / purge      → clean()
//   bi init               → init()
//   bi <unknown>          → print "Unknown command: <x>. Run 'bi --help'.", exit 1
```

**Zero-dependency argument parsing:** Do NOT use `commander`, `yargs`, or `citty`. Parse `process.argv` manually. The command set is small and fixed.

---

## 10. AI Agent Integration

When `--json` flag is present on any command, `bi` switches to **machine mode**:
- All output is valid JSON written to stdout.
- No ANSI colors, no spinners, no interactive prompts.
- Exit codes are meaningful: 0 = success, 1 = command failure, 2 = detection failure.

**Example AI workflow:**
```bash
# AI agent runs:
bi i lodash --json
# Gets back:
{"pm":"pnpm","command":"pnpm add lodash","exitCode":0}
```

This allows AI coding agents to:
1. Detect the active package manager without guessing.
2. Install/remove packages through a single uniform interface.
3. Parse structured results without regex.

---

## 11. Test Plan

**Framework:** vitest
**Run:** `npx vitest run`

### 10.1 `tests/detect.test.ts` — Package Manager Detection (12 tests)

```
describe('detectPackageManager')
  ✓ should detect npm from package-lock.json
  ✓ should detect yarn from yarn.lock
  ✓ should detect pnpm from pnpm-lock.yaml
  ✓ should detect bun from bun.lockb
  ✓ should detect bun from bun.lock
  ✓ should prefer lockfile over packageManager field
  ✓ should use packageManager field when no lockfile exists
  ✓ should parse version from packageManager field ("pnpm@8.15.0")
  ✓ should fall back to npm when nothing found
  ✓ should return reason='lockfile' when detected from lockfile
  ✓ should return reason='packageManager-field' when detected from field
  ✓ should return reason='default' when falling back
```

**Test strategy:** Use `fs.mkdtempSync` to create temp directories with specific lockfiles, then run `detectPackageManager(tempDir)`.

### 10.2 `tests/install.test.ts` — Install Command (6 tests)

```
describe('install')
  ✓ should generate correct npm install command (no packages)
  ✓ should generate correct pnpm add command (with packages)
  ✓ should add -D flag for devDependencies
  ✓ should add -g flag for global install
  ✓ should return JSON output when json=true
  ✓ should map yarn global correctly
```

**Test strategy:** Mock `spawnPM` to capture the generated command string without actually running it.

### 10.3 `tests/remove.test.ts` — Remove Command (4 tests)

```
describe('remove')
  ✓ should generate correct npm uninstall command
  ✓ should generate correct yarn remove command
  ✓ should generate correct pnpm remove command
  ✓ should generate correct bun remove command
```

### 10.4 `tests/run.test.ts` — Run Command (5 tests)

```
describe('run')
  ✓ should generate npm run <script>
  ✓ should generate yarn <script> (no "run" prefix)
  ✓ should generate pnpm <script>
  ✓ should generate bun run <script>
  ✓ should handle shortcut aliases (dev, build, test, start)
```

### 10.5 `tests/exec.test.ts` — Exec Command (4 tests)

```
describe('exec')
  ✓ should generate npx <pkg>
  ✓ should generate yarn dlx <pkg>
  ✓ should generate pnpm dlx <pkg>
  ✓ should generate bunx <pkg>
```

### 10.6 `tests/upgrade.test.ts` — Upgrade Command (4 tests)

```
describe('upgrade')
  ✓ should identify outdated packages
  ✓ should compare semver correctly
  ✓ should output JSON when json=true
  ✓ should handle empty dependencies gracefully
```

**Test strategy:** Mock `https.get` to return a fake npm registry response.

### 10.7 `tests/clean.test.ts` — Clean Command (4 tests)

```
describe('clean')
  ✓ should identify node_modules for deletion
  ✓ should identify cache directories
  ✓ should support dry-run mode
  ✓ should report freed bytes
```

**Test strategy:** Create temp dirs with fake node_modules, run clean, verify deletion.

### 11.8 `tests/cli.test.ts` — CLI Routing (8 tests)

```
describe('CLI router')
  ✓ should route "i" to install
  ✓ should route "rm" to remove
  ✓ should route "dev" to run("dev")
  ✓ should route "x" to exec
  ✓ should print version with --version
  ✓ should print version with -v
  ✓ should print help with --help
  ✓ should print help with -h
```

### 10.9 `tests/spawn.test.ts` — Spawn Utility (3 tests)

```
describe('spawnPM')
  ✓ should use shell:true on Windows
  ✓ should inherit stdio by default
  ✓ should pipe stdio when requested
```

**Total: ~50 test cases**

## 12. package.json (Final)

```json
{
  "name": "bi",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "The 2-letter dev toolkit. Type less, do more.",
  "main": "dist/cli.js",
  "bin": { "bi": "dist/cli.js" },
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "tsup --watch",
    "build": "tsup",
    "test": "vitest run"
  },
  "keywords": ["cli","toolkit","npm","yarn","pnpm","bun","package-manager","dev"],
  "author": "10iii <zh@ngxil.in> (https://zha.ngxil.in)",
  "repository": { "type": "git", "url": "git+https://github.com/10iii/bi.git" },
  "homepage": "https://github.com/10iii/bi#readme",
  "bugs": { "url": "https://github.com/10iii/bi/issues" },
  "license": "MIT",
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/node": "^20.0.0",
    "tsup": "^8.0.0",
    "typescript": "^5.0.0",
    "vitest": "^1.0.0"
  }
}
```

## 13. README.md (Draft)

```markdown
<div align="center">
  <h1>bi</h1>
  <p><b>The 2-letter dev toolkit. Type less, do more.</b></p>
  <p>
    <code>bi</code> auto-detects your package manager and gives you
    a single, ultra-short command for everything.
  </p>
</div>

## Quick Start

    npx bi i          # install deps (auto-detects npm/yarn/pnpm/bun)
    npx bi dev        # run dev server
    npx bi x vitest   # execute a package
    npx bi up         # check for outdated deps
    npx bi clean      # nuke node_modules & caches

## For AI Agents

    bi i lodash --json
    # → {"pm":"pnpm","command":"pnpm add lodash","exitCode":0}

Every command supports `--json` for structured, machine-readable output.
```
